member.name: T:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientDiagnostics
member.summary: Snapshot of the native bridge build, exposed through . Use in health checks to programmatically prove the handler is NOT using Schannel.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientDiagnostics.SslVersion
member.summary: Active TLS backend string, e.g. "OpenSSL/3.6.3 (Schannel)". The first entry is the backend in use; parenthesized entries are compiled-in alternates that this bridge pins away from.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientDiagnostics.TlsBackendIsOpenSsl
member.summary: True when the ACTIVE TLS backend is OpenSSL.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientDiagnostics.NativeLibraryPath
member.summary: Full path curl_http_bridge.dll was loaded from.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientDiagnostics.TrustSource
member.summary: Trust source in effect: the CA bundle path, "(embedded cacert.pem)", and/or "(windows certificate store)".

member.name: T:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions
member.summary: Handler-lifetime configuration for . All values are fixed at handler construction (init-only), mirroring how freezes its options on first use.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.NativeLibraryPath
member.summary: Explicit path (file or directory) of curl_http_bridge.dll. When null the library is resolved from runtimes\win-x64\native\ beside the application. PATH and the current directory are never searched. Process-wide: the first handler wins.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.CertificateAuthorityBundlePath
member.summary: Path of a PEM CA bundle used for server certificate verification. When null, the bundled cacert.pem deployed next to the native bridge is used; if that is also missing, must be enabled.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.UseSystemCertificateStore
member.summary: Additionally trust the Windows certificate store (CAs are imported into OpenSSL's verification, TLS itself stays on OpenSSL). Note that on Windows Server 2012 R2 the store's roots may be outdated, which is why the bundled CA file is the default trust source.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.MinimumTlsVersion
member.summary: Minimum negotiated TLS version. Only 1.2 (default) and 1.3 are accepted; anything older is refused by this handler.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.Tls12CipherList
member.summary: Optional OpenSSL cipher list for TLS 1.2 and below (e.g. "ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256"). Leave null to use OpenSSL's secure defaults.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.Tls13CipherSuites
member.summary: Optional TLS 1.3 cipher suites (e.g. "TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256"). Leave null to use OpenSSL's secure defaults.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.ClientCertificatePath
member.summary: Client certificate file (PEM or PKCS#12) for mutual TLS.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.ClientCertificateType
member.summary: "PEM" (default) or "P12".

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.ClientCertificateKeyPath
member.summary: Private key file when is a PEM certificate without an embedded key.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.ClientCertificateKeyPassword
member.summary: Passphrase for the client key / PKCS#12 file.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.ConnectTimeout
member.summary: Maximum time to establish the TCP/TLS connection. Zero/default lets libcurl use its own default (300 s).

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.RequestTimeout
member.summary: Optional cap on the WHOLE transfer (headers + body). Null (default) leaves total-timeout ownership to , which already cancels via the cancellation token.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.AllowAutoRedirect
member.summary: Follow 3xx redirects natively. Matching .NET semantics, https→http downgrades are never followed.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.AutomaticDecompression
member.summary: Advertise and transparently decode gzip/deflate/brotli. Content-Encoding/Content-Length are stripped from decoded responses, matching .

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.Proxy
member.summary: Explicit proxy. Resolved per request URI via /. Environment proxy variables (HTTP_PROXY etc.) are NEVER honored.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.UseProxy
member.summary: Master switch for . When false, or when is null, requests go direct.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.EnableHttp2
member.summary: Negotiate HTTP/2 via ALPN. Off by default: with the current blocking-transfer architecture each request owns its own connection, so h2 provides no multiplexing benefit and HTTP/1.1 keep-alive pooling is usually faster. Enable for servers that require h2.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.EnableCookieEngineForRedirects
member.summary: Enable libcurl's cookie engine scoped to a single request chain, so cookies set by a redirect hop are replayed on the next hop (SSO flows). Cookies never persist across requests. Off by default; Set-Cookie headers are always surfaced on the response either way.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.EnableNativeVerboseLogging
member.summary: Forward libcurl's verbose TEXT/HEADER diagnostics to the logger at Debug level. Authorization/Cookie header values are redacted; request/response bodies are never logged.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.RedactQueryStringsInLogs
member.summary: Redact query strings when URLs are logged.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.MaxConcurrentRequests
member.summary: Maximum concurrent in-flight requests. Each in-flight request (including one whose response body is still being streamed) owns one dedicated worker thread — size this at least as large as the expected number of concurrently open response streams. Default: max(8, 2 × processor count).

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.WorkerAdmissionTimeout
member.summary: Fail fast with an when no worker becomes available within this window (prevents silent queue build-up behind stuck streaming responses).

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.MaxConnectionsPerServer
member.summary: Best-effort cap of concurrent requests per scheme+host+port, enforced in managed code. 0 = unlimited.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.MaxResponseBufferBytes
member.summary: Upper bound of undelivered response-body bytes buffered between the native transfer and the consumer of the response stream. When full, the native transfer is paused by backpressure.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.ReceiveBufferSize
member.summary: libcurl receive buffer size (bigger chunks = fewer native→managed transitions on fast downloads).

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.PooledConnectionIdleTimeout
member.summary: Idle keep-alive connections older than this are not reused (CURLOPT_MAXAGE_CONN). Zero = libcurl default (118 s).

member.name: P:CurlHttp.CurlHttpClientOptions.PooledConnectionLifetime
member.summary: Connections older than this are never reused regardless of activity (CURLOPT_MAXLIFETIME_CONN); bounds DNS-failover blindness. Zero = unlimited.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.CurlHttpMessageHandler
member.summary:
An that executes HTTP/HTTPS requests through a bundled native libcurl + OpenSSL bridge instead of the OS TLS stack. Built for hosts whose Schannel cannot negotiate modern TLS (Windows Server 2012 R2), while application code keeps using HttpClient.
Lifetime: create ONE handler per distinct configuration and keep it for the application lifetime (like SocketsHttpHandler). The handler is safe for unlimited concurrent SendAsync calls. When used with IHttpClientFactory, set the handler lifetime to infinite — connection staleness is bounded by , so factory recycling only churns native resources.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.CurlHttpMessageHandler.GetDiagnostics
member.summary: Runtime proof of what the native stack actually is. Health checks should assert .

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Diagnostics.CurlHttpEventSource
member.summary: ETW/EventPipe counters and events for the handler. Listen with: dotnet-trace collect --providers CurlHttpClient.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Diagnostics.CurlHttpEventSourceScope
member.summary: Per-request diagnostics scope: one place that fans out to the EventSource and the optional ILogger, with redaction applied once.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Diagnostics.LogRedaction.RedactHeaderLine(System.String)
member.summary: Redacts the value of credential-bearing header lines in verbose native output. Bodies never reach this path (dropped in the native bridge).

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Diagnostics.LogRedaction.RedactUrl(System.Uri,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Optionally strips query strings from URLs before logging (query values frequently carry tokens).

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Internal.BoundedByteQueue
member.summary:
Bounded producer/consumer byte queue between the native transfer thread (synchronous producer) and the response stream consumer (asynchronous reader).
Design constraints (these are what make the streaming design deadlock-free): - The producer blocks when the buffer is full — that block IS the backpressure that pauses libcurl — but the wait is ALWAYS wakeable by . A consumer that stops reading and disposes the stream must be able to unblock a producer stuck inside the native write callback, because libcurl's progress callback cannot fire while control is inside our callback. - The consumer never blocks a thread: waits are TaskCompletionSource-based. - Chunks live in ArrayPool buffers; the pool gets them back as soon as the consumer copies them out (or the queue is drained on abort).

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.BoundedByteQueue.Write(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Blocking write from the native worker thread. Returns false when the transfer must abort (queue aborted by consumer/cancellation).

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.BoundedByteQueue.Complete
member.summary: Producer signals a clean end of body.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.BoundedByteQueue.Fault(System.Exception)
member.summary: Producer signals the transfer failed. Buffered data remains readable; the error surfaces once the buffer is drained (matching how socket-based handlers surface mid-body failures).

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.BoundedByteQueue.Abort(System.Exception)
member.summary: Consumer-side hard stop (stream disposed / caller cancelled). Wakes a blocked producer immediately and releases all buffered data.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.Internal.BoundedByteQueue.BufferedBytes
member.summary: Bytes currently buffered (diagnostics/tests).

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlErrorMapper
member.summary: Bridge result -> .NET exception mapping, following HttpClient conventions: - caller cancellation -> TaskCanceledException carrying the caller token - total request timeout -> TaskCanceledException with TimeoutException inner - connect timeout -> HttpRequestException(ConnectionError) w/ TimeoutException inner - TLS / certificate -> HttpRequestException(SecureConnectionError) w/ AuthenticationException inner - DNS -> HttpRequestException(NameResolutionError) - everything protocol -> HttpRequestException(InvalidResponse) Native detail (curl error code + libcurl error buffer) is preserved in the message and in Exception.Data["CurlErrorCode"] — never any credentials.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlHttpInitializationException
member.summary: Thrown when the native bridge or its libcurl build cannot be initialized (missing DLL, wrong architecture, wrong TLS backend).

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext
member.summary:
Per-request state hub. Lives from SendAsync until the native transfer has fully finished AND the response stream has been consumed or disposed.
Threading map: - SendAsync thread: constructs, prepares the body stream, awaits . - Worker thread: builds the native request, blocks in send; all native callbacks (header/body/read/seek/debug) arrive on this thread. - Any thread: (caller token, handler dispose, response-stream dispose).

member.name: P:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext.OnFinished
member.summary: Set by the dispatcher; releases pool/origin slots when the worker is completely done with this request.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext.ContentLength
member.summary: Signals to the worker whether the request carries a body and its length: long.MinValue none, -1 unknown/streamed, >= 0 known.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext.PrepareRequestBodyAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Opens the request content stream on the SendAsync thread so stream faults surface as clean managed exceptions before any native work starts.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext.Cancel
member.summary: Requests prompt abortion of the transfer from any thread. Wakes: a producer blocked on the full body queue, a body reader blocked on the request stream, and (within ~1 s) libcurl's progress callback for the phases in between.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext.OnBodyData(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Body chunk from libcurl. Returns 0 to continue, 1 to abort.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext.OnHeaderLine(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},CurlHttp.Native.HeaderLineFlags,System.Int32)
member.summary: Raw header line from libcurl. Returns 0 to continue, 1 to abort.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext.OnReadBody(System.Span{System.Byte})
member.summary: Request-body pull from libcurl. Returns bytes produced, 0 at EOF, -1 to abort.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext.OnSeekBody(System.Int64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Rewind request for libcurl (auth retry / redirect resubmit). 0 ok, 1 cannot seek, 2 fail.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext.CompleteTransfer(CurlHttp.Native.CurlBridgeResult,CurlHttp.Native.BridgeResponseInfoNative@,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Called once when the blocking send returns.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext.TryFailFastIfCancelled
member.summary: Worker fast-path: skip the native transfer entirely when the request was cancelled while queued.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext.FailTransfer(System.Exception)
member.summary: Worker-side failure outside the native send (marshaling, native request creation).

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext.OnWorkerFinished
member.summary: Cleanup after the worker is fully done with native resources.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Internal.NativeRequestPlan
member.summary: Everything the worker needs to build the native request — precomputed on the SendAsync path so the worker only marshals.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.NativeRequestPlan.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int64)
member.summary: Everything the worker needs to build the native request — precomputed on the SendAsync path so the worker only marshals.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestDispatcher
member.summary:
Option A execution engine: a bounded pool of dedicated background threads, each running one blocking native transfer end-to-end. Blocking these threads is by design — it is what provides upload/download backpressure — and they are never ThreadPool threads.
Capacity model: admission is gated by a semaphore sized to the pool, so at most MaxConcurrentRequests transfers are in flight; a request whose response body is still streaming continues to own its thread. Admission beyond capacity fails fast after WorkerAdmissionTimeout.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestDispatcher.RunRequest(CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestContext,CurlHttp.Internal.NativeRequestPlan)
member.summary: Runs one transfer end-to-end on this dedicated thread.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlRequestDispatcher.Dispose
member.summary: Stops admission, cancels all in-flight transfers, and joins the workers so the native client can be destroyed safely.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlResponseContent
member.summary: HttpContent over the live native transfer. Never buffers the body itself: with HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead the caller streams directly; with the default completion option HttpClient calls LoadIntoBufferAsync, which drives SerializeToStreamAsync below.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Internal.CurlResponseStream
member.summary: Read-only, forward-only view over the bounded body queue. Data is delivered as the native transfer produces it; disposing the stream while the transfer is still running aborts it (matching SocketsHttpHandler).

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Internal.ResponseHeaderParser
member.summary:
Assembles response header blocks from the raw header lines libcurl delivers, and decides when a block is the FINAL response (the one that becomes the caller's HttpResponseMessage).
libcurl forwards every header block of the transfer through the same callback: informational 1xx responses, each hop of a followed redirect chain, and trailers (proxy CONNECT responses are suppressed natively). The finality rules are deliberately conservative:
- 1xx blocks are consumed and discarded. - Trailer lines (arriving after the first body byte) are collected separately for HttpResponseMessage.TrailingHeaders. - A completed block is IMMEDIATELY final when nothing can supersede it: any 2xx/4xx/5xx, or a 3xx when redirects are disabled or without a Location header. This is what makes ResponseHeadersRead return before the body arrives. - A completed 3xx block with a Location header while redirects are enabled is AMBIGUOUS (libcurl may or may not follow it, e.g. redirect budget, protocol restrictions). It is parked; the next status line discards it, and the first body byte or the end of the transfer promotes it to final. Safe because libcurl never delivers body bytes for a response it acts on.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.Internal.ResponseHeaderParser.HasFinalBlock
member.summary: Set when a completed block is known to be the final response. The owner turns this into the HttpResponseMessage.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.Internal.ResponseHeaderParser.CurrentUri
member.summary: Final URI after managed-tracked redirects.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.ResponseHeaderParser.OnHeaderLine(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},CurlHttp.Native.HeaderLineFlags,System.Int32)
member.summary: Processes one raw header line. Returns true when this line completed the FINAL header block (the caller should publish the response now).

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.ResponseHeaderParser.OnBodyStarted
member.summary: First body byte arrived: whatever block we hold is final.

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Internal.ResponseHeaderParser.OnTransferCompleted
member.summary: Transfer finished without body bytes: promote a parked block.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Native.CurlBridgeResult
member.summary: Mirror of the native curl_bridge_result enum.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Native.HeaderLineFlags
member.summary: Header-line flags mirroring the CURL_BRIDGE_HEADER_* constants.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Native.BridgeClientOptionsNative
member.summary: Mirror of curl_bridge_client_options. All pointer fields reference memory that only needs to live for the duration of the curl_bridge_client_create call (the bridge deep-copies).

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Native.BridgeCallbacksNative
member.summary: Mirror of curl_bridge_callbacks.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Native.BridgeResponseInfoNative
member.summary: Mirror of curl_bridge_response_info.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Native.CurlBridgeClientHandle
member.summary: Owns a native curl_bridge_client*. Release blocks briefly until no request is actively using the client (native backstop); the handler drains its worker pool before disposing this handle.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Native.CurlBridgeRequestHandle
member.summary: Owns a native curl_bridge_request*. The SafeHandle ref-counting guarantees the native object cannot be destroyed while a P/Invoke (including the blocking send and cross-thread cancel) is using it.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Native.NativeCallbacks
member.summary:
The [UnmanagedCallersOnly] trampolines libcurl calls into (via the bridge). These are static function pointers — nothing here can be collected or relocated by the GC, which removes the classic delegate-lifetime hazard. The per-request state travels as a GCHandle in the context pointer.
Contract: never let an exception escape (it would cross two native frames); every failure is recorded on the context and reported to libcurl through the return value.

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Native.NativeLibraryLoader
member.summary:
Controlled resolution of curl_http_bridge.dll.
Security posture: the library is only ever loaded from an absolute path — either one explicitly configured through or a well-known location relative to this assembly. The OS default search order (current directory, PATH) is never used, so a planted DLL cannot be picked up. Once loaded, the module stays loaded for the process lifetime.

member.name: P:CurlHttp.Native.NativeLibraryLoader.LoadedPath
member.summary: Full path the module was actually loaded from (diagnostics).

member.name: M:CurlHttp.Native.NativeLibraryLoader.Configure(System.String)
member.summary: Called by the handler before the first P/Invoke. Validates platform/architecture and pins the configured path. A second handler configuring a different explicit path after the library is loaded is an error (one native module per process).

member.name: T:CurlHttp.Native.NativeMethods
member.summary: P/Invoke surface of curl_http_bridge.dll. The library is resolved through — never from PATH or the current working directory.
